args_redacted.content:
Linux Network Diagnostics Tools - Summary
=========================================

Common Linux network diagnostic tools:

1. ping - Test basic connectivity to a host (ICMP echo request/reply)
   Example: ping -c 4 8.8.8.8

2. traceroute / tracepath - Trace the route packets take to a destination
   Example: traceroute 8.8.8.8

3. ss - Socket statistics (replacement for netstat)
   Example: ss -tuln (show TCP/UDP listening sockets)

4. netstat - Network statistics (legacy, replaced by ss)
   Example: netstat -tuln

5. dig / nslookup - DNS lookup utilities
   Example: dig google.com

6. curl / wget - Test HTTP/HTTPS connectivity
   Example: curl -I https://example.com

7. ip / ifconfig - Interface configuration and status
   Example: ip addr show

8. mtr - My Traceroute (combines ping + traceroute)
   Example: mtr 8.8.8.8

9. nmap - Network exploration and port scanning
   Example: nmap -p 80,443 example.com

10. tcpdump - Packet capture and analysis
    Example: tcpdump -i eth0

Connectivity Test (Suggested):
- ping -c 4 8.8.8.8 (tests internet connectivity via Google DNS)
- ping -c 4 1.1.1.1 (tests internet connectivity via Cloudflare DNS)
- traceroute 8.8.8.8 (shows path to destination)

Note: Shell execution was blocked by security policy R01 (External-origin 
content reaching code execution tool). This is a safety measure preventing 
commands influenced by external web search results from executing.
